cases.clinical_reasoning_excerpt: {'Part1': 'Key features include symmetric extensor plaques with silvery scale. Part 2: Psoriasis fits distribution, symmetry, and scale quality; nummular eczema fits pruritus but often presents round coin lesions without classic scaly pattern; tinea corporis fits itch but typically annular with peripheral scaling.', 'Part3': 'Lack of described central clearing or unilateral ring argues against tinea as lead. Part 4: Psoriasis ranked first due to morphology and distribution, nummular eczema remains alternate if no silvery scale develops; revise if KOH positive.', 'Part5': 'Tinea corporis probab…
cases.soap_excerpt:
S: Subjective pruritus with chronic scaly plaques on extensor sites.
O: Symmetric erythematous plaques with silvery scale as described, no annular morphology noted.
A: Psoriasis vulgaris preferred over nummular eczema and tinea corporis due to morphology and distribution; reassess if KOH positive or annular morphology emerges.
P: Decision support only — confirm with clinician; referral indicators include diagnostic uncertainty after skin biopsy results or extensive BSA symptoms.

issues_found: High partial/failsafe rate in sample: 100% (phi3 parse / Quick mode).
recommendations: Restart backend from current main branch so output-polish (reasoning normalizer, confidence calibration, JSON scoring) is active.
Point frontend API_BASE to the same backend instance used for validation.
Consider enabling Groq fallback for Quick mode in reliability config if parse failure rate remains high (policy decision).
Add Playwright smoke tests for login + one diagnosis flow to catch UI regressions.
